title: Tragacantha macrobotrys
taxonomy: Tragacantha macrobotrys is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. uses: Tragacantha macrobotrys, also known as Astragalus gummifer, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. intro: Tragacantha macrobotrys, also known as Astragalus membranaceus, is a perennial plant in the legume family that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, making it useful for treating conditions such as respiratory infections, cardiovascular disease, and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha macrobotrys include saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacantha macrobotrys are teas, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and reduce inflammation.
